title: Sequence teacher distillation into GBDT
summary: A recent F1 pit-stop paper reports competitive deep sequential models for pit timing, while your strongest runs are still tree ensembles.
rationale: A recent F1 pit-stop paper reports competitive deep sequential models for pit timing, while your strongest runs are still tree ensembles. A good competition pattern is to train a diverse sequence model, then distill its OOF signal into GBDT rather than submit the neural net directly.
implementation_hint: Train a lightweight causal sequence model per driver-stint or driver-race prefix, using only past laps and categorical embeddings for driver/race/compound. Generate fold-local OOF probabilities or penultimate-layer embeddings, then add them as features to CatBoost/XGBoost or blend ranks only after OOF generation.
expected_effect: Adds a genuinely different learner that may capture temporal motifs like gradual tyre fade plus abrupt pace loss better than hand-crafted rolling features.
risk: Easy to leak future laps if prefixes are built incorrectly; standalone net may underperform, so only OOF distillation is worth trusting.
